text.title: feature-discovery Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for feature-discovery as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Building every feature request without prioritization.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Feature bloat reduces the product's core value proposition; every added feature increases maintenance cost and user confusion.

failure-mode.description: No validation step between RICE scoring and engineering commitment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: RICE scores are estimates; without behavioral validation, the team builds on assumptions, not evidence.

failure-mode.description: Copying a competitor feature without understanding why they built it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A competitor feature may exist for a different customer segment or job; copying it without context wastes effort and may confuse your users.

failure-mode.description: Weighting feature requests equally regardless of customer segment value.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A feature requested by 50 free-tier users may be less valuable than one requested by 5 enterprise customers; segment value must factor into prioritization.

failure-mode.description: Generating RICE confidence scores without explicit evidence assessment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent-generated confidence scores are opinions, not evidence assessments; confidence must reflect the quality and quantity of actual validation data collected.
